_note: Claim II: the frontier normalization log|Omega'|-R log|K|=o(N) cannot hold as printed with T subset K (|K|>=N) and R asymp N: the offset is -Theta(N log N) = -omega(N). Finite toys sit ON the balance R*=log|Omega|/log q with offsets straddling 0.

tension.needed_for_o_N: R log|K| = log|Omega'| + o(N) = O(N), impossible since R log|K| >= kappa N log N = omega(N)

two_repairs: (A) two-field reading: the moment columns live in a POINT field E>=N (cf. def:fourier-flat-prefix-leaf, T subset E, Psi(M) in E^w); the normalization |K| is the FIXED base field B with log|B|=O(1) (bytes). The single symbol K conflates E (>=N) with B (O(1)); thm:logmoment-equivalence's analog log C(n,m)-w log|B|=o(n) forces w asymp n only when log|B|=O(1).

(B) one-field reading: keep one K but replace 'R asymp N' by 'R log|K| asymp N', i.e. R=Theta(N/log|K|)=o(N). Then |Omega'| ~ |K|^R=exp(Theta(N))<=3^N is achievable and the printed normalization holds; the over-strong clause is 'R asymp N'.
span_cell_mandatoriness: The span cell is asymptotically mandatory exactly when |K|=O(1) and R asymp N (reading (A)'s bounded base field B, or the |K|=O(1) branch of (B)): then #red={j in [1,R): p|j} = Theta(N), index = |K|^#red = exp(Theta(N)), and Gamma_ell is inflated by index^{ell-1} = exp(Theta(N ell)) -- the atom's own trigger exp(eta N ell). Under the |K|->inf, R=o(N) branch the index is |K|^{o(N)} and the cell may be sub-exponential.
